GeKC and cK are related in the context of ensuring zero mass is assigned to triples violating K. GeKC ensures that the support of ϕpc is 'cut' by the indicator function cK. This relationship is illustrated in Fig. 4.6.
The indicator function cK ensures that zero mass is assigned to triples violating the logical constraint K.

The circuit cK encodes domain constraints and is used in conjunction with a GeKC ϕpc to assign probabilities. The circuit is constructed as a smooth and decomposable circuit, sometimes referred to as a constraint or logical circuit.

GeKC ϕpc and constraint circuit cK are related as they enable efficient computation of ZK, as mentioned in Theorem 2. The partition function ZK of the product of GeKC ϕpc and cK can be computed efficiently.

Constraint circuit cK encodes logical constraint K over variables {S, R, O}, as described in Theorem 2. Computing the partition function ZK involves the product of GeKC ϕpc(s, r, o) · cK(s, r, o) ∝ pK(s, r, o).
Constraint circuit cK is used for encoding domain constraints K, resulting in a compact size circuit, as demonstrated in Prop. A.1.
The domain constraints encoding for ogbl-biokg results in a constraint circuit cK of size approximately 307 · 103.
Comparing the size of constraint circuit cK for ogbl-biokg with the one for COMPLEX with embedding size 1000, the latter is much larger at 375 · 106.
Efficiency argument suggests that integration of logical constraints with GeKCs has negligible overhead due to the compact size of the constraint circuit cK, as obtained via squaring (Section 4.3).

The representation and learning of widely used KGE models like CP, RESCAL, TUCKER, and COMPLEX are re-interpreted as generative models, suggesting a different perspective on their functionality.
GeKC-variants can be devised for other KGE models whose scores are multilinear maps, implying a relationship between the variants and the KGE models.
KGE models defining score functions with distance-based semantics such as TransE and RotatE can be reinterpreted as GeKCs, indicating a shift in the interpretation of these models.

The rich literature on learning the structure of circuits can be leveraged to devise smaller and sparser KGE circuit architectures, suggesting a potential improvement in capturing triple distribution or structural properties for efficient reasoning tasks.
